   145→    ),
   146→    index("idx_adjustment_events_session_id").on(
   147→      table.sessionId,
   148→      table.createdAt,
   149→    ),
   150→  ],
   151→);
   152→
   153→export const messageDeletions = sqliteTable(
   154→  "message_deletions",
   155→  {
   156→    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
   157→    importId: text("import_id")
   158→      .notNull()
   159→      .references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
   160→    messageId: text("message_id").notNull(),
   161→    reason: text("reason"),
   162→    deletedAt: text("deleted_at").notNull(),
   163→  },
   164→  (table) => [
   165→    uniqueIndex("idx_message_deletions_import_message").on(
   166→      table.importId,
   167→      table.messageId,
   168→    ),
   169→  ],
   170→);
   171→
   172→export type Import = typeof imports.$inferSelect;
   173→export type NewImport = typeof imports.$inferInsert;
   174→export type ImportSnapshot = typeof importSnapshots.$inferSelect;
   175→export type NewImportSnapshot = typeof importSnapshots.$inferInsert;
   176→export type AdjustmentSessionRecord = typeof adjustmentSessions.$inferSelect;
   177→export type AdjustmentMessageRecord = typeof adjustmentMessages.$inferSelect;
   178→export type FormatRuleRecord = typeof formatRules.$inferSelect;
   179→export type AdjustmentEventRecord = typeof adjustmentEvents.$inferSelect;
   180→export type MessageDeletionRecord = typeof messageDeletions.$inferSelect;

1→import fs from "node:fs";
     2→import path from "node:path";
     3→import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
     4→
     5→import Database from "better-sqlite3";
     6→import { drizzle } from "drizzle-orm/better-sqlite3";
     7→
     8→import "../load-env.js";
     9→import * as schema from "./schema.js";
    10→
    11→const defaultDbPath = fileURLToPath(
    12→  new URL("../../../../data/chat-exporter.db", import.meta.url),
    13→);
    14→
    15→export const databasePath =
    16→  process.env.CHAT_EXPORTER_DB_PATH &&
    17→  process.env.CHAT_EXPORTER_DB_PATH.trim().length > 0
    18→    ? path.resolve(process.env.CHAT_EXPORTER_DB_PATH)
    19→    : defaultDbPath;
    20→
    21→fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(databasePath), { recursive: true });
    22→
    23→const sqlite = new Database(databasePath);
    24→
    25→sqlite.pragma("journal_mode = WAL");
    26→sqlite.pragma("foreign_keys = ON");
    27→sqlite.pragma("busy_timeout = 5000");
    28→
    29→export const db = drizzle(sqlite, { schema });
    30→export const rawDb = sqlite;
    31→
    32→export function withTransaction<T>(fn: () => T): T {
    33→  return rawDb.transaction(fn)();
    34→}
    35→
    36→// Note: sqlite.exec is the better-sqlite3 API for DDL — no shell invocation.
    37→sqlite.exec(`
    38→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS imports (
    39→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    40→    source_url TEXT NOT NULL,
    41→    source_platform TEXT NOT NULL,
    42→    mode TEXT NOT NULL,
    43→    status TEXT NOT NULL,
    44→    current_stage TEXT NOT NULL,
    45→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    46→    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    47→    warnings_json TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
    48→    error TEXT,
    49→    summary_json TEXT,
    50→    conversation_json TEXT,
    51→    artifacts_json TEXT
    52→  );
    53→
    54→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_imports_created_at ON imports (created_at DESC);
    55→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_imports_status ON imports (status);
    56→
    57→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS import_snapshots (
    58→    import_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    59→    source_url TEXT NOT NULL,
    60→    final_url TEXT NOT NULL,
    61→    fetched_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    62→    page_title TEXT NOT NULL,
    63→    raw_html TEXT NOT NULL,
    64→    normalized_payload_json TEXT NOT NULL,
    65→    fetch_metadata_json TEXT NOT NULL,
    66→    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
    67→  );
    68→
    69→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS adjustment_sessions (
    70→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    71→    import_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    72→    target_format TEXT NOT NULL,
    73→    status TEXT NOT NULL,
    74→    selection_json TEXT NOT NULL,
    75→    preview_artifact_json TEXT,
    76→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    77→    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
    78→  );
    79→
    80→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_adjustment_sessions_import_id
    81→    ON adjustment_sessions (import_id, created_at DESC);
    82→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_adjustment_sessions_target_format
    83→    ON adjustment_sessions (target_format, updated_at DESC);
    84→
    85→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS adjustment_messages (
    86→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    87→    session_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES adjustment_sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    88→    role TEXT NOT NULL,
    89→    content TEXT NOT NULL,
    90→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL
    91→  );
    92→
    93→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_adjustment_messages_session_id
    94→    ON adjustment_messages (session_id, created_at ASC);
    95→
    96→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS format_rules (
    97→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    98→    import_id TEXT REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    99→    target_format TEXT NOT NULL,
   100→    kind TEXT NOT NULL,
   101→    scope TEXT NOT NULL,
   102→    status TEXT NOT NULL,
   103→    selector_json TEXT NOT NULL,
   104→    instruction TEXT NOT NULL,
   105→    compiled_rule_json TEXT,
   106→    source_session_id TEXT REFERENCES adjustment_sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   107→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
   108→    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
   109→  );
   110→
   111→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_format_rules_import_id
   112→    ON format_rules (import_id, target_format, status, created_at DESC);
   113→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_format_rules_profile
   114→    ON format_rules (target_format, status, created_at DESC);
   115→
   116→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS adjustment_events (
   117→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   118→    import_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   119→    session_id TEXT REFERENCES adjustment_sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   120→    rule_id TEXT REFERENCES format_rules(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   121→    target_format TEXT NOT NULL,
   122→    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
   123→    payload_json TEXT,
   124→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL
   125→  );
   126→
   127→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_adjustment_events_import_id
   128→    ON adjustment_events (import_id, target_format, created_at DESC);
   129→  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_adjustment_events_session_id
   130→    ON adjustment_events (session_id, created_at DESC);
   131→
   132→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS message_deletions (
   133→    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   134→    import_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   135→    message_id TEXT NOT NULL,
   136→    reason TEXT,
   137→    deleted_at TEXT NOT NULL
   138→  );
   139→
   140→  CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_message_deletions_import_message
   141→    ON message_deletions (import_id, message_id);
   142→`);
   143→
   144→// Migration: make format_rules.import_id nullable (SQLite cannot ALTER COLUMN)
   145→// Uses a table-rebuild approach because SQLite does not support DROP NOT NULL.
   146→const importIdColumn = sqlite
   147→  .prepare(
   148→    `SELECT "notnull" FROM pragma_table_info('format_rules') WHERE name = 'import_id'`,
   149→  )
   150→  .get() as { notnull: number } | undefined;
   151→
   152→if (importIdColumn && importIdColumn.notnull === 1) {
   153→  // Per SQLite docs, PRAGMA foreign_keys is a no-op inside a transaction, so it
   154→  // must be issued outside one. We therefore split the migration into three
   155→  // separate sqlite.exec calls:
   156→  //   1. Pragma off  (outside transaction)
   157→  //   2. DDL block wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT — crash-safe: a failure between
   158→  //      DROP TABLE and RENAME rolls back automatically, preserving all data.
   159→  //   3. Pragma on   (outside transaction)
   160→  sqlite.exec("PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF;");
   161→
   162→  sqlite.exec(`
   163→    BEGIN;
   164→
   165→    -- Create the new table with nullable import_id
   166→    CREATE TABLE format_rules_new (
   167→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   168→      import_id TEXT REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   169→      target_format TEXT NOT NULL,
   170→      kind TEXT NOT NULL,
   171→      scope TEXT NOT NULL,
   172→      status TEXT NOT NULL,
   173→      selector_json TEXT NOT NULL,
   174→      instruction TEXT NOT NULL,
   175→      compiled_rule_json TEXT,
   176→      source_session_id TEXT REFERENCES adjustment_sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   177→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
   178→      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
   179→    );
   180→
   181→    -- Copy data from old table to new
   182→    INSERT INTO format_rules_new SELECT * FROM format_rules;
   183→
   184→    -- Drop original and rename new to final name
   185→    DROP TABLE format_rules;
   186→    ALTER TABLE format_rules_new RENAME TO format_rules;
   187→
   188→    -- Recreate indexes
   189→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_format_rules_import_id
   190→      ON format_rules (import_id, target_format, status, created_at DESC);
   191→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_format_rules_profile
   192→      ON format_rules (target_format, status, created_at DESC);
   193→
   194→    COMMIT;
   195→  `);
   196→
   197→  sqlite.exec("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;");
   198→
   199→  // Verify FK integrity after migration
   200→  const fkErrors = sqlite.pragma("foreign_key_check") as unknown[];
   201→  if (fkErrors.length > 0) {
   202→    throw new Error(
   203→      `FK-Integritätsprüfung nach Migration fehlgeschlagen: ${JSON.stringify(fkErrors)}`,
   204→    );
   205→  }
   206→}
   207→
   208→// Migration: add error_stage column to imports table
   209→// Note: sqlite.exec is the better-sqlite3 DDL API — no shell invocation.
   210→const errorStageColumn = sqlite
   211→  .prepare(
   212→    `SELECT name FROM pragma_table_info('imports') WHERE name = 'error_stage'`,
   213→  )
   214→  .get() as { name: string } | undefined;
   215→
   216→if (!errorStageColumn) {
   217→  sqlite.exec(`ALTER TABLE imports ADD COLUMN error_stage TEXT`);
   218→}
   219→

1→# Testing Best Practices — Chat Exporter
     2→
     3→> Verbindliche Referenz für alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
     4→> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** — es enthält die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.
     5→
     6→## Tech-Stack
     7→
     8→| Bereich | Tool | Version |
     9→|---------|------|---------|
    10→| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
    11→| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
    12→| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
    13→| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | — |
    14→| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |
    15→
    16→## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing
    17→
    18→**Lies nur die Module, die für deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**
    19→
    20→| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
    21→|---------|-------------------|
    22→| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
    23→| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
    24→| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
    25→| **Code-Review durchführen** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
    26→| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
    27→| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
    28→| **Test-Kategorien wählen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
    29→| **Verfügbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gültig)
    34→
    35→### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung
    36→
    37→Tests prüfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.
    38→
    39→```typescript
    40→// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
    41→test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
    42→  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })
    43→
    44→  const result = generatePreview(selection)
    45→
    46→  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
    47→  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
    48→})
    49→
    50→// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
    51→test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
    52→  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
    53→  generatePreview(createSelection())
    54→  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
    55→})
    56→```
    57→
    58→### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)
    59→
    60→Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:
    61→
    62→```typescript
    63→test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
    64→  // Arrange
    65→  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }
    66→
    67→  // Act
    68→  const result = createSelection(overrides)
    69→
    70→  // Assert
    71→  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
    72→  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
    73→  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
    74→})
    75→```
    76→
    77→### Given-When-Then (für komplexe Szenarien)
    78→
    79→```typescript
    80→test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
    81→  /**
    82→   * Given: Eine aktive Session für importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
    83→   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
    84→   * Then: Bestehende Session zurückgegeben, keine neue erstellt
    85→   */
    86→})
    87→```
    88→
    89→### Tests als Dokumentation
    90→
    91→Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
    92→- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
    93→- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
    94→- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)
    99→
   100→| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
   101→|--------------|-------------|
   102→| Implementierungsdetails testen | Prüfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
   103→| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | Prüfe spezifische Werte |
   104→| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo möglich |
   105→| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | Prüfe nur Schema-Konformität |
   106→| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
   107→| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
   108→| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) für Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |
   109→
   110→---
   111→
   112→## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)
   113→
   114→| Element | Format | Beispiel |
   115→|---------|--------|----------|
   116→| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
   117→| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
   118→| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
   119→| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
   120→| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
   121→| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
   122→| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Detail-Module
   127→
   128→| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
   129→|-------|------|--------|
   130→| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
   131→| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
   132→| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
   133→| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
   134→| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
   135→| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
   136→| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
   137→| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, Ausführungsbefehle |
   138→| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, häufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |
   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Änderungen an Test-Praktiken müssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.
   143→

1→# Testing Best Practices — Chat Exporter
     2→
     3→> Verbindliche Referenz für alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
     4→> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** — es enthält die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.
     5→
     6→## Tech-Stack
     7→
     8→| Bereich | Tool | Version |
     9→|---------|------|---------|
    10→| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
    11→| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
    12→| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
    13→| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | — |
    14→| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |
    15→
    16→## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing
    17→
    18→**Lies nur die Module, die für deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**
    19→
    20→| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
    21→|---------|-------------------|
    22→| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
    23→| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
    24→| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
    25→| **Code-Review durchführen** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
    26→| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
    27→| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
    28→| **Test-Kategorien wählen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
    29→| **Verfügbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gültig)
    34→
    35→### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung
    36→
    37→Tests prüfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.
    38→
    39→```typescript
    40→// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
    41→test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
    42→  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })
    43→
    44→  const result = generatePreview(selection)
    45→
    46→  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
    47→  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
    48→})
    49→
    50→// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
    51→test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
    52→  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
    53→  generatePreview(createSelection())
    54→  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
    55→})
    56→```
    57→
    58→### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)
    59→
    60→Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:
    61→
    62→```typescript
    63→test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
    64→  // Arrange
    65→  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }
    66→
    67→  // Act
    68→  const result = createSelection(overrides)
    69→
    70→  // Assert
    71→  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
    72→  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
    73→  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
    74→})
    75→```
    76→
    77→### Given-When-Then (für komplexe Szenarien)
    78→
    79→```typescript
    80→test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
    81→  /**
    82→   * Given: Eine aktive Session für importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
    83→   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
    84→   * Then: Bestehende Session zurückgegeben, keine neue erstellt
    85→   */
    86→})
    87→```
    88→
    89→### Tests als Dokumentation
    90→
    91→Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
    92→- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
    93→- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
    94→- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)
    99→
   100→| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
   101→|--------------|-------------|
   102→| Implementierungsdetails testen | Prüfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
   103→| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | Prüfe spezifische Werte |
   104→| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo möglich |
   105→| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | Prüfe nur Schema-Konformität |
   106→| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
   107→| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
   108→| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) für Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |
   109→
   110→---
   111→
   112→## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)
   113→
   114→| Element | Format | Beispiel |
   115→|---------|--------|----------|
   116→| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
   117→| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
   118→| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
   119→| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
   120→| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
   121→| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
   122→| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Detail-Module
   127→
   128→| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
   129→|-------|------|--------|
   130→| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
   131→| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
   132→| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
   133→| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
   134→| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
   135→| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
   136→| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
   137→| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, Ausführungsbefehle |
   138→| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, häufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |
   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Änderungen an Test-Praktiken müssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.
   143→

## Task: Implement Step 1 — Shared Types: Snapshot-Schema

Follow strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement, then refactor.
Commit your changes atomically when tests pass (stage files by name, never `git add .`).

### Context

This is a TypeScript monorepo. The shared package is at `packages/shared/`.

**Existing file to extend:** `packages/shared/src/snapshots.ts` — currently has Import-Snapshot schemas (importSnapshotSchema etc.). Add the new ConversationSnapshot schema here.

**Existing file to extend:** `packages/shared/src/contract.ts` — has oRPC contract definitions. Add placeholder snapshot endpoints.

**Index file:** `packages/shared/src/index.ts` re-exports everything from `./snapshots.js`.

### What to implement

1. In `packages/shared/src/snapshots.ts`, add a new `conversationSnapshotSchema` Zod schema:
   - Fields: `id` (string), `importId` (string), `label` (string), `isActive` (number, 0 or 1), `createdAt` (string), `updatedAt` (string)
   - NO `conversationJson` field in this schema (too big for API responses, loaded separately)
   - Export the schema and TypeScript type `ConversationSnapshot` via `z.infer<>`

2. In `packages/shared/src/contract.ts`, add placeholder snapshot endpoints to the contract:
   ```ts
   snapshots: {
     list: oc.input(z.object({ importId: z.string() })).output(z.array(conversationSnapshotSchema)),
     get: oc.input(z.object({ id: z.string() })).output(conversationSnapshotSchema.extend({ conversationJson: z.string() })),
     getActive: oc.input(z.object({ importId: z.string() })).output(conversationSnapshotSchema.extend({ conversationJson: z.string() }).nullable()),
     create: oc.input(z.object({ importId: z.string(), label: z.string(), conversationJson: z.string() })).output(conversationSnapshotSchema),
     activate: oc.input(z.object({ id: z.string() })).output(conversationSnapshotSchema),
     deactivate: oc.input(z.object({ importId: z.string() })).output(z.object({ deactivated: z.boolean() })),
     delete: oc.input(z.object({ id: z.string() })).output(z.object({ deleted: z.boolean() })),
     rename: oc.input(z.object({ id: z.string(), label: z.string() })).output(conversationSnapshotSchema),
     updateContent: oc.input(z.object({ id: z.string(), conversationJson: z.string() })).output(conversationSnapshotSchema),
   }
   ```

3. Import `conversationSnapshotSchema` in contract.ts from `./snapshots.js`.

### Tests

Write tests in `packages/shared/src/__tests__/snapshots.test.ts` (create directory if needed):

```ts
describe("ConversationSnapshotSchema", () => {
  test("validates a complete valid snapshot")
  test("rejects snapshot without importId")
  test("rejects snapshot with invalid isActive value") 
  test("does NOT include conversationJson in schema")
});
```

Test patterns from project:
- Use `import { describe, expect, test } from "vitest"`
- Use `safeParse` for validation tests
- AAA pattern (Arrange-Act-Assert)

Run tests with: `cd "/Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter" && pnpm --filter @chat-exporter/shared test`

### Commit

After tests pass, stage only relevant files and commit:
```
feat(shared): add ConversationSnapshot schema and contract endpoints

Add Zod schema for conversation snapshots (edit versioning)
and register placeholder oRPC endpoints for snapshot CRUD.

Plan step 1 of plan-2.3-kontext-editor.
```

Here is a summary of what was done:

**Step 5 is complete.** All files were already committed in `e0a7803` by a parallel agent. I verified and fixed the implementation to work correctly with the installed TipTap v3.20.1 package structure:

**Key adaptation:** TipTap v3.20.1 bundles all table extensions as named exports from `@tiptap/extension-table` (not default exports from separate packages). The extensions file uses `import { Table, TableRow, TableHeader, TableCell } from "@tiptap/extension-table"` instead of separate default imports.

**Similarly,** the individual StarterKit sub-extension packages (paragraph, heading, etc.) are no longer installed separately -- they're bundled inside `@tiptap/starter-kit`. The node files were adjusted to be comment-only customization points rather than re-exports of non-existent packages.

## Task: Implement Step 3 — Repository: Snapshot CRUD

Follow strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement, then refactor.
Commit your changes atomically when tests pass (stage files by name, never `git add .`).

### Context

Server app at `apps/server/`. Uses Drizzle ORM with better-sqlite3.

**DB client:** `apps/server/src/db/client.ts` exports `db` (drizzle instance), `rawDb` (better-sqlite3 instance), `withTransaction(fn)`.

**Schema:** `apps/server/src/db/schema.ts` has:
```ts
export const conversationSnapshots = sqliteTable("conversation_snapshots", {
  id: text("id").primaryKey(),
  importId: text("import_id").notNull().references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
  label: text("label").notNull(),
  conversationJson: text("conversation_json").notNull(),
  isActive: text("is_active").notNull().default("0"),
  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
  updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
}, (table) => [index("idx_conversation_snapshots_import_id").on(table.importId)]);
```

NOTE: `isActive` is stored as TEXT "0" or "1" (SQLite pattern used in this project).

**Existing repository pattern** (from `delete-repository.ts`):
```ts
import { db, withTransaction } from "../db/client.js";
import { conversationSnapshots } from "../db/schema.js";
import { eq, and } from "drizzle-orm";

// Use db.select().from(table).where(...).all() for queries
// Use db.insert(table).values({...}).run() for inserts
// Use db.update(table).set({...}).where(...).run() for updates
// Use db.delete(table).where(...).run() for deletes
// Use crypto.randomUUID() for IDs
// Use new Date().toISOString() for timestamps
```

### What to implement

Create `apps/server/src/lib/snapshot-repository.ts` with these functions:

1. `createSnapshot(importId: string, label: string, conversationJson: string): ConversationSnapshot`
   - Generates UUID, sets isActive="0", creates timestamps
   - Returns the metadata (without conversationJson) matching the shared ConversationSnapshot type
   - Return type should have `isActive` as number (parse "0"/"1" to 0/1)

2. `listSnapshots(importId: string): ConversationSnapshot[]`
   - Returns all snapshots for an import, WITHOUT conversationJson
   - Parse isActive text to number

3. `getSnapshot(snapshotId: string): (ConversationSnapshot & { conversationJson: string }) | null`
   - Returns full snapshot with conversationJson, or null

4. `getActiveSnapshot(importId: string): (ConversationSnapshot & { conversationJson: string }) | null`
   - Returns the active snapshot (isActive="1") or null

5. `activateSnapshot(snapshotId: string): ConversationSnapshot`
   - In a transaction: deactivate all snapshots for the same importId, then set this one to isActive="1"
   - Return updated snapshot metadata

6. `deactivateAllSnapshots(importId: string): void`
   - Set isActive="0" for all snapshots of this import

7. `deleteSnapshot(snapshotId: string): boolean`
   - Delete and return true if deleted, false if not found

8. `renameSnapshot(snapshotId: string, label: string): ConversationSnapshot | null`
   - Update label and updatedAt, return updated metadata or null

9. `updateSnapshotContent(snapshotId: string, conversationJson: string): ConversationSnapshot | null`
   - Update conversationJson and updatedAt, return metadata or null

Helper to convert DB row to ConversationSnapshot (parse isActive):
```ts
function toSnapshot(row: ConversationSnapshotRecord): ConversationSnapshot {
  return {
    id: row.id,
    importId: row.importId,
    label: row.label,
    isActive: row.isActive === "1" ? 1 : 0,
    createdAt: row.createdAt,
    updatedAt: row.updatedAt,
  };
}
```

### Tests

Write tests in `apps/server/src/lib/snapshot-repository.test.ts`.

Follow the existing test pattern from `import-repository.test.ts`:
- Tests use the REAL database (not in-memory)
- Clean up created data in `afterEach`
- Use factory functions for test data
- Import from vitest: `import { afterEach, describe, expect, test } from "vitest"`

You need a parent import for FK. Create a helper:
```ts
import { db } from "../db/client.js";
import { imports } from "../db/schema.js";

function createTestImport(): string {
  const id = crypto.randomUUID();
  db.insert(imports).values({
    id,
    sourceUrl: "https://example.com",
    sourcePlatform: "chatgpt",
    mode: "archive",
    status: "completed",
    currentStage: "done",
    createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
  }).run();
  return id;
}
```

Test cases (from validation workflow):
```ts
describe("snapshot-repository", () => {
  test("createSnapshot creates a new snapshot with correct fields")
  test("listSnapshots returns metadata without conversationJson")
  test("getSnapshot returns full snapshot with conversationJson")
  test("getActiveSnapshot returns null when no snapshot is active")
  test("activateSnapshot sets isActive=1 and deactivates others")
  test("deactivateAllSnapshots sets all snapshots to isActive=0")
  test("deleteSnapshot removes the snapshot")
  test("renameSnapshot updates the label")
  test("updateSnapshotContent updates conversationJson")
  test("activateSnapshot is atomic (transaction)")
});
```

Run tests: `cd "/Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter" && pnpm --filter @chat-exporter/server test`

### Commit

```
feat(server): add snapshot repository with CRUD operations

Implement createSnapshot, listSnapshots, getSnapshot, getActiveSnapshot,
activateSnapshot, deactivateAllSnapshots, deleteSnapshot, renameSnapshot,
and updateSnapshotContent with transaction support.

Plan step 3 of plan-2.3-kontext-editor.
```

**DB client:** `apps/server/src/db/client.ts` exports `db` (drizzle instance), `rawDb` (better-sqlite3 instance), `withTransaction(fn)`.

**Schema:** `apps/server/src/db/schema.ts` has:
```ts
export const conversationSnapshots = sqliteTable("conversation_snapshots", {
  id: text("id").primaryKey(),
  importId: text("import_id").notNull().references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
  label: text("label").notNull(),
  conversationJson: text("conversation_json").notNull(),
  isActive: text("is_active").notNull().default("0"),
  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
  updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
}, (table) => [index("idx_conversation_snapshots_import_id").on(table.importId)]);
```

NOTE: `isActive` is stored as TEXT "0" or "1" (SQLite pattern used in this project).

**Existing repository pattern** (from `delete-repository.ts`):
```ts
import { db, withTransaction } from "../db/client.js";
import { conversationSnapshots } from "../db/schema.js";
import { eq, and } from "drizzle-orm";

// Use db.select().from(table).where(...).all() for queries
// Use db.insert(table).values({...}).run() for inserts
// Use db.update(table).set({...}).where(...).run() for updates
// Use db.delete(table).where(...).run() for deletes
// Use crypto.randomUUID() for IDs
// Use new Date().toISOString() for timestamps
```

### What to implement

Create `apps/server/src/lib/snapshot-repository.ts` with these functions:

1. `createSnapshot(importId: string, label: string, conversationJson: string): ConversationSnapshot`
   - Generates UUID, sets isActive="0", creates timestamps
   - Returns the metadata (without conversationJson) matching the shared ConversationSnapshot type
   - Return type should have `isActive` as number (parse "0"/"1" to 0/1)

2. `listSnapshots(importId: string): ConversationSnapshot[]`
   - Returns all snapshots for an import, WITHOUT conversationJson
   - Parse isActive text to number

3. `getSnapshot(snapshotId: string): (ConversationSnapshot & { conversationJson: string }) | null`
   - Returns full snapshot with conversationJson, or null

4. `getActiveSnapshot(importId: string): (ConversationSnapshot & { conversationJson: string }) | null`
   - Returns the active snapshot (isActive="1") or null

5. `activateSnapshot(snapshotId: string): ConversationSnapshot`
   - In a transaction: deactivate all snapshots for the same importId, then set this one to isActive="1"
   - Return updated snapshot metadata

6. `deactivateAllSnapshots(importId: string): void`
   - Set isActive="0" for all snapshots of this import

7. `deleteSnapshot(snapshotId: string): boolean`
   - Delete and return true if deleted, false if not found

8. `renameSnapshot(snapshotId: string, label: string): ConversationSnapshot | null`
   - Update label and updatedAt, return updated metadata or null

9. `updateSnapshotContent(snapshotId: string, conversationJson: string): ConversationSnapshot | null`
   - Update conversationJson and updatedAt, return metadata or null

Helper to convert DB row to ConversationSnapshot (parse isActive):
```ts
function toSnapshot(row: ConversationSnapshotRecord): ConversationSnapshot {
  return {
    id: row.id,
    importId: row.importId,
    label: row.label,
    isActive: row.isActive === "1" ? 1 : 0,
    createdAt: row.createdAt,
    updatedAt: row.updatedAt,
  };
}
```

### Tests

Write tests in `apps/server/src/lib/snapshot-repository.test.ts`.

Follow the existing test pattern from `import-repository.test.ts`:
- Tests use the REAL database (not in-memory)
- Clean up created data in `afterEach`
- Use factory functions for test data
- Import from vitest: `import { afterEach, describe, expect, test } from "vitest"`

You need a parent import for FK. Create a helper:
```ts
import { db } from "../db/client.js";
import { imports } from "../db/schema.js";

function createTestImport(): string {
  const id = crypto.randomUUID();
  db.insert(imports).values({
    id,
    sourceUrl: "https://example.com",
    sourcePlatform: "chatgpt",
    mode: "archive",
    status: "completed",
    currentStage: "done",
    createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
  }).run();
  return id;
}
```

Test cases (from validation workflow):
```ts
describe("snapshot-repository", () => {
  test("createSnapshot creates a new snapshot with correct fields")
  test("listSnapshots returns metadata without conversationJson")
  test("getSnapshot returns full snapshot with conversationJson")
  test("getActiveSnapshot returns null when no snapshot is active")
  test("activateSnapshot sets isActive=1 and deactivates others")
  test("deactivateAllSnapshots sets all snapshots to isActive=0")
  test("deleteSnapshot removes the snapshot")
  test("renameSnapshot updates the label")
  test("updateSnapshotContent updates conversationJson")
  test("activateSnapshot is atomic (transaction)")
});
```

Run tests: `cd "/Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter" && pnpm --filter @chat-exporter/server test`

   156→
   157→// Migration: make format_rules.import_id nullable (SQLite cannot ALTER COLUMN)
   158→// Uses a table-rebuild approach because SQLite does not support DROP NOT NULL.
   159→const importIdColumn = sqlite
   160→  .prepare(
   161→    `SELECT "notnull" FROM pragma_table_info('format_rules') WHERE name = 'import_id'`,
   162→  )
   163→  .get() as { notnull: number } | undefined;
   164→
   165→if (importIdColumn && importIdColumn.notnull === 1) {
   166→  // Per SQLite docs, PRAGMA foreign_keys is a no-op inside a transaction, so it
   167→  // must be issued outside one. We therefore split the migration into three
   168→  // separate sqlite.exec calls:
   169→  //   1. Pragma off  (outside transaction)
   170→  //   2. DDL block wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT — crash-safe: a failure between
   171→  //      DROP TABLE and RENAME rolls back automatically, preserving all data.
   172→  //   3. Pragma on   (outside transaction)
   173→  sqlite.exec("PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF;");
   174→
   175→  sqlite.exec(`
   176→    BEGIN;
   177→
   178→    -- Create the new table with nullable import_id
   179→    CREATE TABLE format_rules_new (
   180→      id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   181→      import_id TEXT REFERENCES imports(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   182→      target_format TEXT NOT NULL,
   183→      kind TEXT NOT NULL,
   184→      scope TEXT NOT NULL,
   185→      status TEXT NOT NULL,
   186→      selector_json TEXT NOT NULL,
   187→      instruction TEXT NOT NULL,
   188→      compiled_rule_json TEXT,
   189→      source_session_id TEXT REFERENCES adjustment_sessions(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
   190→      created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
   191→      updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
   192→    );
   193→
   194→    -- Copy data from old table to new
   195→    INSERT INTO format_rules_new SELECT * FROM format_rules;
   196→
   197→    -- Drop original and rename new to final name
   198→    DROP TABLE format_rules;
   199→    ALTER TABLE format_rules_new RENAME TO format_rules;
   200→
   201→    -- Recreate indexes
   202→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_format_rules_import_id
   203→      ON format_rules (import_id, target_format, status, created_at DESC);
   204→    CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_format_rules_profile
   205→      ON format_rules (target_format, status, created_at DESC);
   206→
   207→    COMMIT;
   208→  `);
   209→
   210→  sqlite.exec("PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;");
   211→
   212→  // Verify FK integrity after migration
   213→  const fkErrors = sqlite.pragma("foreign_key_check") as unknown[];
   214→  if (fkErrors.length > 0) {
   215→    throw new Error(
   216→      `FK-Integritätsprüfung nach Migration fehlgeschlagen: ${JSON.stringify(fkErrors)}`,
   217→    );
   218→  }
   219→}

   223→const errorStageColumn = sqlite

1→# Testing Best Practices — Chat Exporter
     2→
     3→> Verbindliche Referenz für alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
     4→> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** — es enthält die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.
     5→
     6→## Tech-Stack
     7→
     8→| Bereich | Tool | Version |
     9→|---------|------|---------|
    10→| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
    11→| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
    12→| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
    13→| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | — |
    14→| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |
    15→
    16→## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing
    17→
    18→**Lies nur die Module, die für deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**
    19→
    20→| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
    21→|---------|-------------------|
    22→| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
    23→| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
    24→| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
    25→| **Code-Review durchführen** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
    26→| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
    27→| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
    28→| **Test-Kategorien wählen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
    29→| **Verfügbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gültig)
    34→
    35→### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung
    36→
    37→Tests prüfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.
    38→
    39→```typescript
    40→// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
    41→test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
    42→  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })
    43→
    44→  const result = generatePreview(selection)
    45→
    46→  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
    47→  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
    48→})
    49→
    50→// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
    51→test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
    52→  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
    53→  generatePreview(createSelection())
    54→  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
    55→})
    56→```
    57→
    58→### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)
    59→
    60→Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:
    61→
    62→```typescript
    63→test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
    64→  // Arrange
    65→  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }
    66→
    67→  // Act
    68→  const result = createSelection(overrides)
    69→
    70→  // Assert
    71→  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
    72→  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
    73→  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
    74→})
    75→```
    76→
    77→### Given-When-Then (für komplexe Szenarien)
    78→
    79→```typescript
    80→test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
    81→  /**
    82→   * Given: Eine aktive Session für importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
    83→   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
    84→   * Then: Bestehende Session zurückgegeben, keine neue erstellt
    85→   */
    86→})
    87→```
    88→
    89→### Tests als Dokumentation
    90→
    91→Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
    92→- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
    93→- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
    94→- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)
    99→
   100→| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
   101→|--------------|-------------|
   102→| Implementierungsdetails testen | Prüfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
   103→| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | Prüfe spezifische Werte |
   104→| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo möglich |
   105→| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | Prüfe nur Schema-Konformität |
   106→| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
   107→| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
   108→| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) für Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |
   109→
   110→---
   111→
   112→## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)
   113→
   114→| Element | Format | Beispiel |
   115→|---------|--------|----------|
   116→| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
   117→| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
   118→| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
   119→| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |
   120→| E2E-Spec | `<feature>.spec.ts` | `import-flow.spec.ts` |
   121→| E2E-Page-Object | `<page>.page.ts` | `format-workspace.page.ts` |
   122→| E2E-Test | `test("<user-szenario>", ...)` | `test("user imports chat and sees workspace", ...)` |
   123→
   124→---
   125→
   126→## Detail-Module
   127→
   128→| Modul | Pfad | Inhalt |
   129→|-------|------|--------|
   130→| **Fixtures** | [`docs/testing/fixtures.md`](docs/testing/fixtures.md) | Factory-Pattern, Test-Daten, bestehende Factories |
   131→| **Mocking** | [`docs/testing/mocking.md`](docs/testing/mocking.md) | Mock-Grenzen, `vi.fn()`, Fetch-Mocking |
   132→| **Server-Patterns** | [`docs/testing/server-patterns.md`](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) | Env-Snapshot, Fetch-Mock, Async-Testing |
   133→| **Frontend** | [`docs/testing/frontend.md`](docs/testing/frontend.md) | Vitest, React Testing Library, TanStack Query |
   134→| **E2E** | [`docs/testing/e2e.md`](docs/testing/e2e.md) | Playwright, Page Objects, Fixtures |
   135→| **Review** | [`docs/testing/review.md`](docs/testing/review.md) | 10-Punkte Review-Checkliste |
   136→| **Coverage** | [`docs/testing/coverage.md`](docs/testing/coverage.md) | Zielwerte, Stufenplan, Messung |
   137→| **Kategorien** | [`docs/testing/categories.md`](docs/testing/categories.md) | Test-Pyramide, Workspace-Filter, Ausführungsbefehle |
   138→| **Flaky-Policy** | [`docs/testing/flaky.md`](docs/testing/flaky.md) | Definition, häufige Ursachen, Beispiel-Fixes |
   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→> **Hinweis**: Dieses Dokument und seine Module sind lebende Dokumente. Bei Änderungen an Test-Praktiken müssen die betroffenen Module aktualisiert werden.
   143→

toolUseResult.prompt:
## Task: Implement Step 3 — Repository: Snapshot CRUD

Follow strict TDD: write failing tests first, then implement, then refactor.
Commit your changes atomically when tests pass (stage files by name, never `git add .`).

### Context

Server app at `apps/server/`. Uses Drizzle ORM with better-sqlite3.

**DB client:** `apps/server/src/db/client.ts` exports `db` (drizzle instance), `rawDb` (better-sqlite3 instance), `withTransaction(fn)`.

**Schema:** `apps/server/src/db/schema.ts` has:
```ts
export const conversationSnapshots = sqliteTable("conversation_snapshots", {
  id: text("id").primaryKey(),
  importId: text("import_id").notNull().references(() => imports.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
  label: text("label").notNull(),
  conversationJson: text("conversation_json").notNull(),
  isActive: text("is_active").notNull().default("0"),
  createdAt: text("created_at").notNull(),
  updatedAt: text("updated_at").notNull(),
}, (table) => [index("idx_conversation_snapshots_import_id").on(table.importId)]);
```

NOTE: `isActive` is stored as TEXT "0" or "1" (SQLite pattern used in this project).

**Existing repository pattern** (from `delete-repository.ts`):
```ts
import { db, withTransaction } from "../db/client.js";
import { conversationSnapshots } from "../db/schema.js";
import { eq, and } from "drizzle-orm";

// Use db.select().from(table).where(...).all() for queries
// Use db.insert(table).values({...}).run() for inserts
// Use db.update(table).set({...}).where(...).run() for updates
// Use db.delete(table).where(...).run() for deletes
// Use crypto.randomUUID() for IDs
// Use new Date().toISOString() for timestamps
```

### What to implement

Create `apps/server/src/lib/snapshot-repository.ts` with these functions:

1. `createSnapshot(importId: string, label: string, conversationJson: string): ConversationSnapshot`
   - Generates UUID, sets isActive="0", creates timestamps
   - Returns the metadata (without conversationJson) matching the shared ConversationSnapshot type
   - Return type should have `isActive` as number (parse "0"/"1" to 0/1)

2. `listSnapshots(importId: string): ConversationSnapshot[]`
   - Returns all snapshots for an import, WITHOUT conversationJson
   - Parse isActive text to number

3. `getSnapshot(snapshotId: string): (ConversationSnapshot & { conversationJson: string }) | null`
   - Returns full snapshot with conversationJson, or null

4. `getActiveSnapshot(importId: string): (ConversationSnapshot & { conversationJson: string }) | null`
   - Returns the active snapshot (isActive="1") or null

5. `activateSnapshot(snapshotId: string): ConversationSnapshot`
   - In a transaction: deactivate all snapshots for the same importId, then set this one to isActive="1"
   - Return updated snapshot metadata

6. `deactivateAllSnapshots(importId: string): void`
   - Set isActive="0" for all snapshots of this import

7. `deleteSnapshot(snapshotId: string): boolean`
   - Delete and return true if deleted, false if not found

8. `renameSnapshot(snapshotId: string, label: string): ConversationSnapshot | null`
   - Update label and updatedAt, return updated metadata or null

9. `updateSnapshotContent(snapshotId: string, conversationJson: string): ConversationSnapshot | null`
   - Update conversationJson and updatedAt, return metadata or null

Helper to convert DB row to ConversationSnapshot (parse isActive):
```ts
function toSnapshot(row: ConversationSnapshotRecord): ConversationSnapshot {
  return {
    id: row.id,
    importId: row.importId,
    label: row.label,
    isActive: row.isActive === "1" ? 1 : 0,
    createdAt: row.createdAt,
    updatedAt: row.updatedAt,
  };
}
```

### Tests

Write tests in `apps/server/src/lib/snapshot-repository.test.ts`.

Follow the existing test pattern from `import-repository.test.ts`:
- Tests use the REAL database (not in-memory)
- Clean up created data in `afterEach`
- Use factory functions for test data
- Import from vitest: `import { afterEach, describe, expect, test } from "vitest"`

You need a parent import for FK. Create a helper:
```ts
import { db } from "../db/client.js";
import { imports } from "../db/schema.js";

function createTestImport(): string {
  const id = crypto.randomUUID();
  db.insert(imports).values({
    id,
    sourceUrl: "https://example.com",
    sourcePlatform: "chatgpt",
    mode: "archive",
    status: "completed",
    currentStage: "done",
    createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
  }).run();
  return id;
}
```

Test cases (from validation workflow):
```ts
describe("snapshot-repository", () => {
  test("createSnapshot creates a new snapshot with correct fields")
  test("listSnapshots returns metadata without conversationJson")
  test("getSnapshot returns full snapshot with conversationJson")
  test("getActiveSnapshot returns null when no snapshot is active")
  test("activateSnapshot sets isActive=1 and deactivates others")
  test("deactivateAllSnapshots sets all snapshots to isActive=0")
  test("deleteSnapshot removes the snapshot")
  test("renameSnapshot updates the label")
  test("updateSnapshotContent updates conversationJson")
  test("activateSnapshot is atomic (transaction)")
});
```

Run tests: `cd "/Users/philippbriese/Documents/dev/chat exporter" && pnpm --filter @chat-exporter/server test`

### Commit

```
feat(server): add snapshot repository with CRUD operations

Implement createSnapshot, listSnapshots, getSnapshot, getActiveSnapshot,
activateSnapshot, deactivateAllSnapshots, deleteSnapshot, renameSnapshot,
and updateSnapshotContent with transaction support.

Plan step 3 of plan-2.3-kontext-editor.
```

1→# Testing Best Practices — Chat Exporter
     2→
     3→> Verbindliche Referenz für alle Tests in diesem Projekt.
     4→> Dieses Dokument ist ein **Smart Index** — es enthält die Kern-Prinzipien inline und verweist auf Detail-Module in `docs/testing/`.
     5→
     6→## Tech-Stack
     7→
     8→| Bereich | Tool | Version |
     9→|---------|------|---------|
    10→| Unit/Integration | **Vitest** | ^4.0 |
    11→| E2E | **Playwright** | ^1.58 |
    12→| Coverage | **@vitest/coverage-v8** | ^4.0 |
    13→| Monorepo | **Turborepo + pnpm** | — |
    14→| Linting | **Biome** | ^2.4 |
    15→
    16→## Aufgabenbasiertes Routing
    17→
    18→**Lies nur die Module, die für deine aktuelle Aufgabe relevant sind:**
    19→
    20→| Aufgabe | Lade diese Module |
    21→|---------|-------------------|
    22→| **Server Unit-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md), [server-patterns](docs/testing/server-patterns.md) |
    23→| **Frontend-Komponenten-Test schreiben** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md), [frontend](docs/testing/frontend.md) |
    24→| **E2E-Test schreiben** | [e2e](docs/testing/e2e.md) |
    25→| **Code-Review durchführen** | [review](docs/testing/review.md), [mocking](docs/testing/mocking.md) |
    26→| **Flaky Test debuggen** | [flaky](docs/testing/flaky.md) |
    27→| **Coverage verbessern** | [coverage](docs/testing/coverage.md) |
    28→| **Test-Kategorien wählen** | [categories](docs/testing/categories.md) |
    29→| **Verfügbare Fixtures finden** | [fixtures](docs/testing/fixtures.md) |
    30→
    31→---
    32→
    33→## Kern-Prinzipien (immer gültig)
    34→
    35→### Verhalten testen, nicht Implementierung
    36→
    37→Tests prüfen **was** eine Funktion tut, nicht **wie** sie es tut.
    38→
    39→```typescript
    40→// RICHTIG: Verhalten testen
    41→test("generatePreview returns formatted markdown for selection", () => {
    42→  const selection = createSelection({ selectedText: "Hello **world**" })
    43→
    44→  const result = generatePreview(selection)
    45→
    46→  expect(result).toContain("Hello")
    47→  expect(result).toMatch(/world/)
    48→})
    49→
    50→// FALSCH: Implementierung testen
    51→test("generatePreview calls internal parser", () => {
    52→  const spy = vi.spyOn(internals, "parseMarkdown")
    53→  generatePreview(createSelection())
    54→  expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalled() // Bricht bei Refactoring
    55→})
    56→```
    57→
    58→### Arrange-Act-Assert (AAA)
    59→
    60→Jeder Test folgt dem AAA-Pattern, visuell getrennt durch Leerzeilen:
    61→
    62→```typescript
    63→test("createSelection merges overrides into defaults", () => {
    64→  // Arrange
    65→  const overrides = { blockIndex: 5, blockType: "heading" as const }
    66→
    67→  // Act
    68→  const result = createSelection(overrides)
    69→
    70→  // Assert
    71→  expect(result.blockIndex).toBe(5)
    72→  expect(result.blockType).toBe("heading")
    73→  expect(result.messageId).toBe("message-1") // Default beibehalten
    74→})
    75→```
    76→
    77→### Given-When-Then (für komplexe Szenarien)
    78→
    79→```typescript
    80→test("adjustment session deduplicates when same importId+format exists", () => {
    81→  /**
    82→   * Given: Eine aktive Session für importId "abc" mit Format "markdown"
    83→   * When: createOrReuse mit gleichen Parametern aufgerufen wird
    84→   * Then: Bestehende Session zurückgegeben, keine neue erstellt
    85→   */
    86→})
    87→```
    88→
    89→### Tests als Dokumentation
    90→
    91→Gute Testnamen lesen sich wie Spezifikationen:
    92→- `"generatePreview strips HTML tags from selection"`
    93→- `"adjustment assistant rejects empty draft messages"`
    94→- `"rule compiler maps heading selector to correct CSS"`
    95→
    96→---
    97→
    98→## Anti-Patterns (immer verboten)
    99→
   100→| Anti-Pattern | Stattdessen |
   101→|--------------|-------------|
   102→| Implementierungsdetails testen | Prüfe das Ergebnis, nicht den internen Call |
   103→| Tautologische Tests (`expect(x).toBeDefined()`) | Prüfe spezifische Werte |
   104→| Overmocking (>3 Mocks pro Test) | Teste gegen echte Implementierung wo möglich |
   105→| Fragile Assertions (exakte Prompt-Strings) | Prüfe nur Schema-Konformität |
   106→| Tests ohne Assertions | Jeder Test braucht mindestens eine verhaltensbasierte Assertion |
   107→| Copy-Paste statt Parametrisierung | `test.each` oder `describe.each` verwenden |
   108→| Feste Timeouts (`setTimeout`) für Sync | `waitFor()` (RTL) oder `expect.poll()` (Playwright) |
   109→
   110→---
   111→
   112→## Namenskonventionen (Kurzreferenz)
   113→
   114→| Element | Format | Beispiel |
   115→|---------|--------|----------|
   116→| Vitest-Testdatei | `<module>.test.ts` | `adjustment-preview.test.ts` |
   117→| Vitest-Komponententest | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
   118→| Vitest-Test | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("rejects empty draft", ...)` |
   119→| Vitest-Gruppe | `describe("<Modul>", ...)` | `describe("AdjustmentPreview", ...)` |

1→# Frontend-Testing-Standards
     2→
     3→> Detail-Referenz — geladen aus `TESTING.md` Index.
     4→
     5→## Stack
     6→
     7→| Komponente | Tool |
     8→|------------|------|
     9→| Test-Runner | **Vitest** |
    10→| Umgebung | **jsdom** |
    11→| Rendering | **React Testing Library** (`@testing-library/react`) |
    12→| User-Events | **userEvent** (`@testing-library/user-event`) |
    13→| State-Management | **TanStack Query** (via `createTestQueryClient()`) |
    14→
    15→> **Status**: Web-App hat noch keine Unit-Tests. Dieses Modul definiert die Standards für zukünftige Tests.
    16→
    17→## Rendering-Pattern
    18→
    19→Alle Komponenten-Tests müssen einen QueryClient-Wrapper verwenden:
    20→
    21→```typescript
    22→import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query"
    23→import { render } from "@testing-library/react"
    24→import type { ReactNode } from "react"
    25→
    26→function createTestQueryClient() {
    27→  return new QueryClient({
    28→    defaultOptions: {
    29→      queries: { retry: false },
    30→      mutations: { retry: false },
    31→    },
    32→  })
    33→}
    34→
    35→function renderWithProviders(ui: ReactNode) {
    36→  const queryClient = createTestQueryClient()
    37→  return render(
    38→    <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>{ui}</QueryClientProvider>,
    39→  )
    40→}
    41→```
    42→
    43→```typescript
    44→// FALSCH: Ohne Provider — schlägt fehl bei Komponenten mit Queries/Mutations
    45→render(<FormatWorkspace />)
    46→```
    47→
    48→## User-Interaktionen
    49→
    50→Immer `userEvent` statt `fireEvent` verwenden — näher am realen Nutzerverhalten:
    51→
    52→```typescript
    53→import userEvent from "@testing-library/user-event"
    54→
    55→test("toggle switches adjustment mode", async () => {
    56→  const user = userEvent.setup()
    57→  renderWithProviders(<AdjustmentToggle />)
    58→
    59→  await user.click(screen.getByRole("switch"))
    60→
    61→  expect(screen.getByRole("switch")).toBeChecked()
    62→})
    63→```
    64→
    65→## Async-Rendering abwarten
    66→
    67→```typescript
    68→import { screen, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"
    69→
    70→test("loads and displays rules", async () => {
    71→  renderWithProviders(<RulesList />)
    72→
    73→  await waitFor(() =>
    74→    expect(screen.getByText("Heading Rule")).toBeInTheDocument(),
    75→  )
    76→})
    77→```
    78→
    79→## Hook-Testing
    80→
    81→TanStack Query Hooks mit `renderHook` testen:
    82→
    83→```typescript
    84→import { renderHook, waitFor } from "@testing-library/react"
    85→
    86→test("fetches adjustment session", async () => {
    87→  const wrapper = ({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) => (
    88→    <QueryClientProvider client={createTestQueryClient()}>
    89→      {children}
    90→    </QueryClientProvider>
    91→  )
    92→
    93→  const { result } = renderHook(() => useAdjustmentSession("abc"), { wrapper })
    94→
    95→  await waitFor(() => expect(result.current.isSuccess).toBe(true))
    96→  expect(result.current.data?.rules).toHaveLength(2)
    97→})
    98→```
    99→
   100→## Radix UI Polyfills
   101→
   102→Komponenten die Radix UI nutzen (shadcn/ui), brauchen Browser-API-Polyfills:
   103→
   104→```typescript
   105→beforeAll(() => {
   106→  Element.prototype.hasPointerCapture ??= vi.fn().mockReturnValue(false)
   107→  Element.prototype.setPointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
   108→  Element.prototype.releasePointerCapture ??= vi.fn()
   109→  Element.prototype.scrollIntoView ??= vi.fn()
   110→})
   111→```
   112→
   113→## Namenskonventionen
   114→
   115→| Element | Format | Beispiel |
   116→|---------|--------|----------|
   117→| Testdatei | `<Component>.test.tsx` | `RulesList.test.tsx` |
   118→| Testdatei (non-React) | `<module>.test.ts` | `format-utils.test.ts` |
   119→| Test-Suite | `describe("<Component>", ...)` | `describe("RulesList", ...)` |
   120→| Einzeltest | `test("<verhalten>", ...)` | `test("renders connected badge when active", ...)` |
   121→
